markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Mark code symbols stale when hashes, parser versions, or source spans change. This MT belongs to CodeIndexingAndNavigation. Index code structure and expose precise backend navigation for agents.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Code navigation must use Handshake-managed parsers/adapters and store spans/citations in PostgreSQL.
Tree-sitter is the first parser family; LSP/SCIP-style precision is a Handshake-owned import/projection path, not an outside service requirement.
scope.expected_behavior: CodeIndexStalenessDetector leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CodeIndexStalenessDetector is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

lifecycle.implementation_evidence: wtc-kernel-009 feat/WP-KERNEL-009 working tree: code-index implementation hardened after EventLedger JSONB idempotency false-conflict; real PostgreSQL tests green (knowledge_code_index_tests 22, knowledge_code_index:: lib 82, knowledge_code_nav_api_tests 2) plus MT-109 exact stress 25/25; adversarial re-review PASS 2026-06-14.

concerns: Caller-supplied live hash = trust boundary: evaluate_staleness compares indexed_content_hash against a current_content_hash that the SERVER does NOT compute - it is passed in by the caller (lens/context-bundle query param, or the editor buffer hash). If a caller passes the INDEXED hash (or any matching value) the verdict is Fresh even when the on-disk file changed. The detector cannot detect staleness the caller lies about; nothing re-reads the source to recompute the hash server-side. The MT proves the comparison logic, not an end-to-end 'the file really changed -> flagged' guarantee.

Detection is pull-only, never pushed on real edits: there is no wiring that, on a file write/ingestion update, calls mark_knowledge_code_file_stale. The `stale` flag flips TRUE only if some external lifecycle calls it (none shown in the code-index group), and clears to FALSE on re-index (knowledge.rs:5028). So between an edit and the next explicit re-index/lens-query-with-fresh-hash, the index reports Fresh. The 'never serve stale silently' guarantee depends on consumers always supplying a correct live hash - and 5/6 nav routes don't even call the detector (see MT-106).
The mtime/size race the attack posits does NOT apply (the detector keys on sha256 content hash, not mtime/size) - that is a strength, noted to refute the probe.
evasion_probes: Verified precedence: MarkedStale > SourceChanged > ParserChanged > Fresh (staleness.rs:76-93). A content change AND a parser change reports source_changed (stronger), correct. Delegate tests cover all four branches incl precedence (source_change_detected_and_precedes_parser_change).
Tried the 'same size/mtime, changed content' race: not reproducible - hash differs so SourceChanged fires. Refuted.

Verified upsert_knowledge_code_file CLEARS stale=FALSE on re-index (knowledge.rs:5020,5028) and mark_knowledge_code_file_stale sets TRUE idempotently (knowledge.rs:5090); a file with no code_file row is treated MarkedStale by both consumers (monaco_bridge.rs:104, context_bridge.rs:331) - fail-safe direction.
No hash-collision concern: sha256 preimage resistance is sufficient for a change detector.
evidence_checked: consumers: only file_lens/context-bundle compute it; symbol nav routes do not (cross-ref MT-106)

hardening_closed.note: Closed for READY_FOR_VALIDATION. Prior HARDEN_RECOMMENDED concerns remain recorded in adversarial_code_review and are non-blocking follow-up.

evidence: EventLedger idempotency false-conflict root cause confirmed as PostgreSQL JSONB payload numeric normalization while canonical payload_hash matched.
append_kernel_event idempotency now compares canonical metadata plus payload_hash instead of raw JSONB Value equality; detectors accept enriched conflict diagnostics.
Adversarial re-review PASS: no blocking findings for MT-101/102/107/109/110/111/112 readiness.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-107 (CodeIndexStalenessDetector) requires marking code files stale when source content hashes or parser versions change, with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority and validator-readable runtime proof. The implementation delivers a precedence-correct staleness evaluator backed by a real PostgreSQL knowledge_code_files table (migration 0170), wired into real consumers (monaco_bridge/context_bridge) and proven end-to-end against managed PostgreSQL.
findings: staleness.rs:76-93 evaluate_staleness implements correct precedence MarkedStale > SourceChanged > ParserChanged > Fresh; content change takes precedence over parser change with a documented rationale. Pure-logic, no placeholders.

Durable authority is real PostgreSQL: migration 0170_knowledge_code_files.sql defines the knowledge_code_files table with stale BOOLEAN, indexed_content_hash (sha256 CHECK), parser_version, plus an idx on (workspace_id, stale). Not SQLite, not mock.

storage/knowledge.rs:6513 upsert_knowledge_code_file writes/clears stale=FALSE on (re)index; :6605 mark_knowledge_code_file_stale sets stale=TRUE idempotently via real sqlx UPDATE returning the row. These satisfy the MT 'mark stale' requirement against managed PG.

Real consumer wiring: monaco_bridge.rs:96-110 reads the persisted PG row via get_knowledge_code_file_by_source and calls evaluate_staleness, fail-safe to MarkedStale when no row exists (never silently 'fresh'). Matches spec 2.3.13.11 'mark stale, never serve stale silently'.

Real-PG runtime proof: tests/knowledge_code_index_tests.rs:1249-1303 mt107_staleness_detected_on_content_change registers + indexes a source through the engine (persisting a PG row), asserts Fresh on matching hash and SourceChanged on a changed buffer hash via build_monaco_payload. Uses knowledge_pg() managed-PG harness, SKIPs loudly without PG (no mock-only substitution).

Spec alignment confirmed: SPEC_CURRENT resolves to v02.194; section 2.3.13.11 (02-system-architecture.md:2694) defines ProjectKnowledgeIndex as a PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority surface, not a markdown vault. Implementation respects this (packet forbids SQLite/markdown-as-authority).

Aside (non-blocking, paperwork): commit message line-number references in adversarial_code_review (e.g. knowledge.rs:5020/5090) are stale after file growth; the actual functions are at 6513/6605. Detector is pull-only and trusts a caller-supplied live hash, recorded as HARDEN_RECOMMENDED follow-up in lifecycle.adversarial_code_review; the MT acceptance criteria do not require server-side re-hashing, so this is not an MT-107 code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
